selected_important_turn.text: It's a bit skewed towards you with all those items isn't it? If you want two waters then I'll need 3 food and one water. You can have all 3 firewood
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is a pivotal counter-offer made right after the opponent moved to an integrative, prosocial allocation. It shifts the interaction back toward competitive bargaining and risks undoing a cooperative resolution that was already forming.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The language is demanding and escalatory (asking for 3 food after the opponent conceded). Because the opponent had just offered a cooperative split, this move could be seen as greedily renegotiating or undermining trust. It was unnecessary to secure a good outcome and could have damaged the chance of smooth agreement.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Accept the opponent's integrative concession or, if you genuinely need to ask for more, frame it as a tentative question or trade-off (acknowledge their concession, explain your constraint briefly, and offer an additional concession in return). Avoid demanding language and present the change as a collaborative problem to solve.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (if you accept or reframe) — otherwise may decrease
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (if you accept/reframe to balance), decrease if left as a demand
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase if accepted/reframed; decrease if insistently demanded
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve if you avoid demanding tone; worsen if you persist with aggressive demand

value2reason.Low: It is a warm summer and we don't need that much firewood.
value2reason.Medium: My kids are diabetic and need water to keep themselves hydrated and to flush their systems of medications.
value2reason.High: My kids are diabetic and need to have extra food.

dialogue.text: Hi, How are you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Good how are things?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: yeah. going good.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: good to hear
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: How about the deal?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Sure. My kids are really looking forward to this camping trip but one has special needs and he has to have a specific diet. I am fairly flexible on the water and firewood but food is a must have for me.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Okay. then I'll choose 2 food, 2 firewood and 1 water
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: You want two food or you're allowing me to have 2 food?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I want 2 food
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: My kids need the food. If you allow me to have two food I'll give you all the firewood
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: okay then I'll choose 1 food 3 firewood, and 2 water
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: It's a bit skewed towards you with all those items isn't it? If you want two waters then I'll need 3 food and one water. You can have all 3 firewood
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I understand your need for food. Let's split the food evenly—1.5 each—but since we can't split packages, I'll take 2 food and you take 1. For firewood, I'll take 2 and you take 1. Water can be split 1-1. That way, we both get what we need.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I appreciate your offer, but I need to prioritize food more. If you take 1 food and 2 water, I’ll take 2 food and 1 water. That way, we both get what we need without compromising on essentials.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I see your point about prioritizing food. Let’s adjust—take 2 food, 1 firewood, and 1 water. You can have 1 food, 2 firewood, and 2 water. That balances our needs and ensures we both have essentials.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand your concerns. Let’s finalize this: I’ll take 2 food, 1 water, and 2 firewood. You can have 1 food, 2 water, and 1 firewood. This way, we both get what we need without compromising on essentials.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I agree to this split. You get 2 food, 2 firewood, and 1 water. I’ll take 1 food, 1 firewood, and 2 water. It’s fair and meets our needs.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I appreciate the agreement, but I need to clarify—do you agree to this split: 2 food, 2 firewood, and 1 water for me, and 1 food, 1 firewood, and 2 water for you? This ensures we both meet our needs and respect the priorities.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I agree to this split. You get 2 food, 2 firewood, and 1 water. I’ll take 1 food, 1 firewood, and 2 water. It’s fair and meets our needs.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I appreciate the agreement, but I need to clarify—do you agree to this split: 2 food, 2 firewood, and 1 water for me, and 1 food, 1 firewood, and 2 water for you? This ensures we both meet our needs and respect the priorities.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
